content.full_text:
September 11, 1991

John Wallace, Attorney
Fair Political Practices Commission

Dear Mr. Wallace,

I am requesting a written letter confirming the application of section 8/102.1(c), Title 2, California Code of Regulations to my potential membership on the Davis School Board.

The facts are these. I am a candidate for a vacancy on the Davis School Board which will be filled by appointment by the School Board on Monday, September 16, 1991. My wife is currently a full-time permanent certificated employee (teacher) who has been employed by the Davis School District for approximately six years.

I would appreciate written confirmation of the oral advice I have received from FPPC that regulation section 18702.1(c) would apply to my situation if I were to be appointed to the Davis School Board. My understanding is that no conflict of interest exists (and I would not be prohibited from participating in negotiations or agreements) setting the salary and other related issues for all employees in the same job classification as my wife. Including the salary of my wife, I would only be prohibited from participating in a small number of unusual situations where a decision would have a unique effect on my wife or in decisions to hire, fire, promote, demote or discipline my wife.

I understand that your written confirmation can only relate to the Political Reform Act and other laws under the jurisdiction of the FPPC.

As the School Board intends to make a decision on Monday evening, September 16, and this matter is of great concern to the Board, I would appreciate a written letter by then, if possible.

I can usually be reached at Davis (916) 758-3142 except on Thursday, Sept 12, where I can be reached at (916) 322-7090.

Thank you very much for your consideration of this request.

Sincerely,

Jorge Carrillo
203 Jalisco Pl
Davis, Calif. 95616
